questions.text: Two planets X and Y travel counterclockwise in circular orbits around a star as shown in the figure below: The radii of their orbits are in the ratio 3 : 1. At some time, they are aligned as in Fig. 1, making a straight line with the star. After five Earth years, the angular displacement of planet X is 90.0°, as in Fig. 2. What is the angular displacement of planet Y at this time?
questions.diagram_description: Two diagrams labeled Fig. 1 and Fig. 2 showing two planets X and Y in circular orbits around a central star. Fig. 1 shows the planets aligned in a straight line with the star. Fig. 2 shows planet X has moved 90° from its initial position.

questions.text: You are holding a bottle of sparkling water inside a car moving forward. When the driver applies the brakes:

choices.a: Bubbles in the middle of the liquid will start to move forward with respect to the bottle.
choices.b: Bubbles will start to move backward with respect to the bottle.
choices.c: Bubbles will stay at the same horizontal location in the water.
choices.d: Depending on the speed of the car, bubbles might move forward or backward.

questions.text: An Earth satellite revolves in a circular orbit at a height h from the surface of the Earth. If R is the Earth's radius and g is the acceleration due to gravity at the surface of the Earth, then the speed of the satellite is given by:

A Russian nuclear icebreaker named 50 Let Pobedy has a length of 160 m, a width of 30 m and two nuclear reactors, each delivering about 30 MW of power. It can clear a path 35 m wide in 2 m thick ice (at −10°C) at a speed of 5 knots (2.5 m/s). Somebody suggested that instead of breaking the ice, one should melt it with a gigantic heater mounted at the front of the ship.

choices.a: For this to work, what power should this heater have, in order to achieve the same performance as the icebreaker?
choices.b: Explain in one sentence whether such a heater would work or not on this icebreaker.

questions.text: You are given a graph of temperature as a function of time of a container with a mixture of ice and water slowly heated at constant rate. When all the ice melts, there is 550 mL of water in the container. In the following questions, neglect the evaporation of water.

choices.a: At which rate is the container heated?
choices.b: How much ice was initially present?

questions.diagram_description: A graph showing temperature (in degrees C) as a function of time. The x-axis shows time in seconds from 0 to 1500, and the y-axis shows temperature from -1.75 to 7.00 degrees C. The graph shows a curved line starting at about 0°C, remaining flat initially, then rising steeply after around 750 seconds in a linear fashion.

questions.text: Assume that sodium produces monochromatic light with a wavelength of 5.89 × 10^{-7}m. At what approximate rate would a 10 watt sodium-vapour light be emitting photons? Assume that the efficiency of the light bulb is about 30%.

Three batteries are placed in series. Each battery has an internal resistance r. If one of the batteries is placed the wrong way around as shown in the picture, what will be the total resistance of the three cells now?
questions.diagram_description: Circuit diagram showing three batteries in series, with one battery oriented in reverse polarity compared to the other two

questions.text: At the designed intensity, the two beams circulating in the Large Hadron Collider (LHC) at CERN consist of 5616 bunches (2808 in each direction) of approximately 1.15 × 10^11 protons per bunch. A small commercial hydrogen cylinder contains 40 L of gas at a pressure of 10 MPa and a temperature of 25°C. Assuming an injection efficiency of 70%, how many times could the LHC be filled at the designed intensity using a single, perfectly hermetic cylinder?
The Earth is constantly receiving energy from the Sun. To stay at approximately the same temperature, the Earth loses energy by:

choices.e: The Earth does not lose energy, this is why we have global warming.

questions.text: A car starts to move at time t = 0. If the engine of the car is able to provide constant power, which of the following statements is correct about the speed of car at the beginning of the motion?

choices.b: The speed increases proportionally to time passed (v ∝ t).
choices.c: The speed increases as the square root of time (v ∝ √t).
choices.d: The speed increases as time squared (v ∝ t²).

questions.text: A detector far away from the source of a wave is detecting pulses of that wave every 0.2 second. If the detector starts to move towards the source at a speed of 6.0 km/h, then it would detect a total of 18200 pulses per hour. What is the speed of the wave?

There are three different liquids, with densities ρ₁, ρ₂ and ρ₃, in a U-shaped container as shown in the picture. The lengths shown are H₁ = 15 cm and H₂ = 10 cm. Which of the following equations is the correct relation between the densities of the fluids in the container?
questions.diagram_description: U-shaped container with three different liquids. The left arm contains liquid ρ₂ at the bottom and liquid ρ₁ above it with height H₂. The right arm contains liquid ρ₃ with height H₁. Heights H₁ = 15 cm and H₂ = 10 cm are marked.

questions.text: The intensity of light from a source decreases with the distance x from the source, as 1/x². In the following picture, the intensity of light from the source S at point A on a curtain is 8.1U where U is some unit. We then add a big mirror parallel to the curtain at point B. The distances AS and SB are equal, and the points A, S and B are in line perpendicular to the curtain and the mirror. What is the intensity of light reaching point A now?
questions.diagram_description: Diagram shows a light source S positioned between a curtain (left) and a mirror (right). Point A is on the curtain, point B is on the mirror. The distances are marked as equal lengths L from S to both the curtain and mirror, with S positioned centrally between them.

questions.text: Which of the following is the correct free-body diagram for a cylinder floating in water?

choices.a: Diagram showing cylinder with upward $F_{buoyant}$ and downward $mg$
choices.b: Diagram showing cylinder with downward $mg$ and upward $F_{buoyant}$

questions.diagram_description: Four diagrams (a-d) showing cylinders with different force vectors: buoyant force and weight in various configurations

questions.text: Vancouver's latitude is about 49°, and Earth's axial tilt is about 23°. The power delivered by the Sun reaching a horizontal surface in Vancouver at noon differs in winter and summer. What is the approximate ratio of this quantity measured in winter, compared to that measured in summer?
Consider a metal rod firmly attached to a wall. When you strike the rod with a hammer, which kind of wave will you excite?

choices.c: Either kind, or both, depending on where and how you hit the rod.
choices.d: No wave will be excited, the metal is too strong.

questions.diagram_description: Simple diagram showing a hammer striking a rod that is attached to a wall

questions.text: Assume that you videotaped the fall of a ball due to gravity in vacuum, and are now playing the video in reverse at the same speed. Which of the following statements is correct about the acceleration of the ball seen in these conditions, compared to that of the actual falling ball?

choices.a: The accelerations are the same in both cases.
choices.b: They have the same value but opposite directions.
choices.c: They have different values but the same direction.
choices.d: Both the values and directions differ.

questions.text: A rod (AB) is attached to a fixed point (C) using a light rope (AC). The other end of the rod (B) is sitting on ice with negligible friction and the system is in stationary position. Which of the following can be the equilibrium configuration of this system?

choices.a: Configuration showing rod at angle with rope
choices.b: Configuration showing rod at different angle with rope
choices.c: Configuration showing rod horizontal with vertical rope
choices.d: Configuration showing rod at steep angle with rope

questions.diagram_description: Four diagrams (a-d) showing different configurations of a rod AB connected to fixed point C by rope, with point B on ice surface

questions.text: Which of the following is the closest to the total mass of the atmosphere around the Earth?

At shallow depth, $h$, the pressure in the ocean is simply given by $P = P_0 + \rho gh$, in which $\rho$ is the density of water and $P_0$ is the air pressure. As we go deeper, the high pressure causes the water to compress and become denser. Which of the following sketches illustrates the correct dependence of the pressure on the depth $h$?
questions.diagram_description: Question includes pressure-depth relationship formula and mentions sketches showing pressure vs depth relationships (diagrams not fully visible on this page)

questions.text: An aluminium plate and a glass plate are left in a room for a long time. Putting one ice cube on each plate, you notice that the ice melts faster on the aluminium plate. Why?

choices.a: The ice is in thermal equilibrium with the glass plate, but not with the aluminium plate.
choices.b: Aluminium conducts heat to the ice more rapidly than glass.
choices.c: The aluminium plate holds more heat.

questions.text: You are on the side of the highway, listening to the siren of a fast-approaching ambulance. When the ambulance is not moving the frequency of its siren is 1000 Hz. Which of the following graphs best describes the frequency that you hear as the ambulance approaches and then passes you?

choices.a: Graph showing frequency starting at about 1040 Hz and decreasing linearly
choices.b: Graph showing frequency starting at about 920 Hz and increasing linearly to about 1080 Hz
choices.c: Graph showing frequency constant at about 1000 Hz with sharp drop to about 920 Hz around time 3
choices.d: Graph showing frequency starting at about 1080 Hz, decreasing gradually, then dropping sharply to about 920 Hz

questions.diagram_description: Four graphs labeled a) through d) showing frequency vs time over 6 seconds, each with different patterns representing the Doppler effect as an ambulance approaches and passes

questions.text: Which of the following radiation types has the longest wavelength?

choices.e: They all have at the same wavelength

questions.text: A cup filled with water has a hole in the side, through which the liquid is flowing out. If the cup is dropped from a height, what will happen to the water flowing from the cup?

choices.a: It will keep coming out, flowing at the same rate as before:
choices.b: It will keep coming out, but it will flow a bit slower than before.
choices.c: It will keep coming out, but start to flow upwards relative to the cup:
choices.d: It will keep coming out, flowing horizontally with respect to the falling cup:

questions.diagram_description: Five diagrams showing a cup with water and a hole in the side, with different water flow patterns corresponding to each answer choice

questions.text: Two cubes, with respective masses $M_1$ and $M_2$ and side lengths $L_1$ and $L_2$, are lying on a smooth table as shown. What is the pressure exerted by the cubes on the table?
questions.diagram_description: Two cubes of different sizes sitting on a table, labeled with masses M₁ and M₂, and side lengths L₁ and L₂

questions.text: Consider an object that floats in water but sinks in oil. When the object floats in water, half of it is submerged. If we slowly pour oil on top of the water so it completely covers the object, the object:
The pressure in a stream of water flowing out of a faucet is:

choices.c: the same everywhere in the stream

questions.diagram_description: Image shows water flowing from a faucet in a stream
